title: Maha Shivratri
description: Celebrating the cosmic dance of Lord Shiva and Parvati.

notification_templates.discovery: Did you know? Offering Bilva leaves to Shiva destroys the sins of 3 lifetimes. Prepare for Mahashivratri.
notification_templates.countdown: Just 7 days until the great night of Shiva! Have you planned your fast and puja?
notification_templates.eve: Tomorrow is Maha Shivaratri! Get ready for the sacred Jagaran.
notification_templates.day_of: Happy Maha Shivaratri! May Lord Shiva bless you with strength, peace, and eternal wisdom. Om Namah Shivaya! 🙏

muhurat.description: The most auspicious window during the night to perform Shiva Puja, offering Bilva leaves and milk.

ritual_steps.title: Purification
ritual_steps.description: Take a vow (Sankalpa) after taking a bath in the morning. Fast strictly for the whole day.

ritual_steps.title: Abhisheka
ritual_steps.description: Perform Rudrabhishek of the Shiva Linga with water, milk, honey, and ghee.

ritual_steps.title: Offerings
ritual_steps.description: Offer Bilva (Bael) leaves, Dhatura flowers, and Bhasma (sacred ash) to Lord Shiva.

ritual_steps.title: Jagaran
ritual_steps.description: Stay awake throughout the night singing bhajans and chanting 'Om Namah Shivaya'.

dress_guide.description: Devotees typically wear simple, clean, unstitched clothes or traditional attire like a dhoti-kurta for men and a simple saree or salwar for women. Green is considered highly auspicious as it represents nature and Bilva leaves.

recipes.name: Sabudana Khichdi
recipes.description: A beloved fasting dish made with tapioca pearls, peanuts, and mild spices.
steps: Soak sabudana overnight or for 4-5 hours until soft but not mushy.
Heat ghee in a pan, add cumin seeds and green chilies.
Add cubed potatoes and sauté for a minute.
Mix in the drained sabudana, crushed peanuts, and rock salt.
Cook on low heat until sabudana turns translucent. Garnish with coriander.

recipes.name: Thandai
recipes.description: A cooling milk-based traditional drink infused with nuts, seeds, and spices.
steps: Soak the nuts, melon seeds, and fennel in warm water for 2 hours.
Blend the soaked ingredients into a smooth paste.
Boil milk, add sugar and the prepared paste.
Simmer for 10 minutes, add cardamom powder, and let it cool. Serve chilled.

historical_significance.fact: Maha Shivratri marks the night Lord Shiva performed the Tandava cosmic dance. Devotees fast and offer Bel leaves, milk, and honey throughout the night.

title: Holi
description: The vibrant festival of colors, celebrating the victory of good over evil.

notification_templates.discovery: Holi Fact: The festival marks the eternal and divine love of Radha and Krishna. Get ready for colors!
notification_templates.countdown: One week left until Holi! Stock up on herbal Gulal and get your Gujiya recipes ready.
notification_templates.eve: Tonight is Holika Dahan. May the fire burn away all negativity. Tomorrow we play colors!
notification_templates.day_of: Happy Holi! 🎨 Wishing you a vibrant, joyous, and colorful day with your loved ones.

muhurat.description: The ideal evening time for lighting the Holika bonfire, signifying the victory of good over evil.

ritual_steps.title: Holika Dahan
ritual_steps.description: Gather around the bonfire on the eve of Holi, offer prayers, and roast stalks of wheat or gram in the fire.

ritual_steps.title: Preparation
ritual_steps.description: Oil your hair and skin in the morning to protect against harsh colors.

ritual_steps.title: Playing Colors
ritual_steps.description: Apply dry Gulal on elders' feet for blessings, and playfully splash wet colors on friends and family.

ritual_steps.title: Feasting
ritual_steps.description: Share traditional sweets like Gujiya and drinks like Thandai with neighbors and loved ones.

dress_guide.description: White is the universally loved color for Holi as it acts as a perfect canvas for the vibrant colors (Gulal) thrown during the day. Avoid expensive or newly bought clothes.

recipes.name: Traditional Gujiya
recipes.description: A classic Holi sweet: deep-fried pastry filled with sweetened khoya and dried fruits.
steps: Knead maida with water and 2 tbsp ghee to make a firm dough. Let it rest.
Roast khoya slightly, let it cool. Mix in sugar, dry fruits, and cardamom.
Roll small dough circles, place a spoonful of filling, fold into half moon, and seal the edges tightly.
Deep fry in medium-hot ghee until golden brown. Serve crisp.

historical_significance.fact: Holi originated in ancient India and marks the victory of Prahlada over the demoness Holika. It celebrates the triumph of devotion over arrogance and evil.

title: Diwali
description: The grand festival of lights celebrating the return of Lord Rama to Ayodhya.

muhurat.description: The Pradosh Kaal mixed with Amavasya tithi, the ideal window to pray to Goddess Lakshmi and Lord Ganesha.

ritual_steps.title: Cleaning (Safai)
ritual_steps.description: Start by thoroughly cleaning the entire house to welcome Goddess Lakshmi.

ritual_steps.title: Decorating
ritual_steps.description: Light Diyas, hang Marigold garlands, and draw vibrant Rangoli at the entrance.

ritual_steps.title: Lakshmi Puja
ritual_steps.description: Place idols of Ganesha and Lakshmi. Offer sweets, coins, and perform Aarti.

ritual_steps.title: Celebration
ritual_steps.description: Distribute sweets to neighbors and burst green crackers safely.

dress_guide.description: Diwali is the grandest festival, calling for heavy, rich, and brand new traditional clothing. Silks, brocades, and bright festive colors are preferred.

recipes.name: Besan Ladoo
recipes.description: Classic Diwali sweet made from roasted gram flour, ghee, and sugar.
steps: Melt ghee in a heavy pan and add besan. Roast on low flame until golden brown and aromatic (approx 20 mins).
Let the mixture cool slightly.
Mix in sugar, cardamom, and nuts.
Take small portions and roll them tightly into round balls (ladoos).

notification_templates.discovery: Diwali marks Lord Rama's return to Ayodhya after 14 years. It is a celebration of inner light.
notification_templates.countdown: Diwali is only a week away! Time to finish the deep cleaning and start making sweets.
notification_templates.eve: Tomorrow is Diwali! Prepare the diyas and the rangoli to welcome Maa Lakshmi.
notification_templates.day_of: Happy Diwali! 🪔✨ May your life be illuminated with endless prosperity, health, and joy.

historical_significance.fact: Diwali commemorates Lord Rama's return to Ayodhya after 14 years of exile and his victory over Ravana. Citizens lit lamps to welcome him back.

title: Navratri & Durga Puja
description: Nine nights celebrating the divine feminine and Goddess Durga.

muhurat.description: The most auspicious window to establish the Kalash on the first day of Sharadiya Navratri.

ritual_steps.title: Ghatasthapana
ritual_steps.description: Sow barley seeds in clay and set up the copper Kalash.

ritual_steps.title: Fasting
ritual_steps.description: Observe a pure Sattvic fast throughout the 9 days.

ritual_steps.title: Garba / Dandiya
ritual_steps.description: Participate in traditional devotional dance rituals in the evening.

ritual_steps.title: Maha Ashtami Kanya Pujan
ritual_steps.description: Worship young girls as the embodiment of Goddess Durga.

recipes.name: Sabudana Vada
recipes.description: Crispy fried tapioca pearl patties often eaten during fasting.
steps: Soak sabudana for 4 hours.
Mash potatoes and mix with sabudana, crushed peanuts, chili, and salt.
Shape into patties and deep fry until golden.

dress_guide.description: Each of the 9 days carries a specific color corresponding to the Navadurga.

notification_templates.discovery: Navratri is not just a dance festival; it's a 9-day journey honoring the divine feminine.
notification_templates.countdown: Sharad Navratri is 7 days away! Ready your Chaniya Cholis and Kurta Pajamas.
notification_templates.eve: Tomorrow begins the 9 divine nights of Navratri! Jai Ambe Maa.
notification_templates.day_of: Happy Navratri! Step into the cosmic rhythm of devotion and Garba.

historical_significance.fact: Navratri is a 9-night festival dedicated to Goddess Durga. Each night honors one of her nine divine forms, culminating in Dussehra on the 10th day.

title: Ganesh Chaturthi
description: Welcoming Lord Ganesha, the remover of obstacles.

muhurat.description: Ganesha is believed to have been born during Madhyahna (midday), making this the most auspicious time for Sthapana.

ritual_steps.title: Pranapratishtha
ritual_steps.description: Install the eco-friendly Ganesha idol on a raised platform during the auspicious time.

ritual_steps.title: Shodashopachara
ritual_steps.description: Perform 16-step worship including offering Durva grass, red Hibiscus flowers, and Modaks.

ritual_steps.title: Aarti
ritual_steps.description: Sing the Ganesh Aarti ('Jai Ganesh Jai Ganesh') with family while ringing bells and offering camphor.

ritual_steps.title: Visarjan
ritual_steps.description: After 1.5, 3, 5, 7, or 10 days, bid farewell and immerse the idol in a water body.

recipes.name: Ukadiche Modak
recipes.description: Steamed sweet dumplings made of rice flour, filled with coconut and jaggery.
steps: Cook coconut and jaggery together until melting. Add cardamom. This is the filling.
Boil 1 cup water with a pinch of salt and ghee. Turn off heat, stir in rice flour, cover for 5 mins.
Knead the hot dough until smooth.
Shape dough into small bowls, fill with the coconut mixture, pinch the edges to form a peak.
Steam for 10-12 minutes. Serve hot with a drizzle of ghee.

dress_guide.description: Traditional Maharashtrian attire like Nauvari sarees for women and Kurta-pajamas with a Pheta (turban) for men are popular. Bright red is Ganesha's favored color.

notification_templates.discovery: Ganesha loves Durva grass! Offering 21 blades of Durva clears obstacles. Prepare for Chaturthi.
notification_templates.countdown: Bappa is arriving in 7 days! Have you booked your eco-friendly idol?
notification_templates.eve: Tomorrow is Ganesh Chaturthi! Clean the mandap and prepare the Modak ingredients.
notification_templates.day_of: Ganpati Bappa Morya! 🐘🌺 Wishing you an auspicious Ganesh Chaturthi filled with wisdom and joy.

historical_significance.fact: Ganesh Chaturthi was popularized as a public festival by Lokmanya Bal Gangadhar Tilak in 1893 to unite Indians during the freedom struggle against British rule.

title: Krishna Janmashtami
description: Celebrating the divine birth of Lord Krishna.

muhurat.description: Midnight marks the exact time of Lord Krishna's birth in Mathura.

ritual_steps.title: Fasting
ritual_steps.description: Observe a strict fast for the entire day until midnight.

ritual_steps.title: Footprints
ritual_steps.description: Draw small footprints from the doorstep to the puja room symbolizing child Krishna entering the house.

ritual_steps.title: Midnight Bathing
ritual_steps.description: At midnight, bathe the idol of Bal Krishna (Laddu Gopal) in Panchamrit.

ritual_steps.title: Swinging & Offering
ritual_steps.description: Dress the idol in new clothes, place Him in a swing, and offer Makhan Mishri.

recipes.name: Makhan Mishri
recipes.description: Lord Krishna's absolute favorite: freshly churned white butter mixed with rock sugar.
ingredients: 1 cup Fresh cream (Malai) or homemade white butter
steps: If using cream, churn it vigorously until the whey separates and the white butter (makhan) gathers.
Collect the butter, wash it gently with chilled water.
Mix in the mishri crystals.
Place a Tulsi leaf on top before offering it to the deity.

dress_guide.description: Bright, festive colors. Children are often dressed up as young Krishna or Radha, complete with a peacock feather resting on the crown.

notification_templates.discovery: Did you know? Krishna's favorite Makhan Mishri represents the purity and sweetness of devotion.
notification_templates.countdown: Only 1 week until Janmashtami! Get your Laddu Gopal's poshak and swing ready.
notification_templates.eve: Tonight is Janmashtami Eve! The midnight bells will ring soon to celebrate His birth.
notification_templates.day_of: Happy Janmashtami! May the melodies of Krishna's flute fill your life with love and joy. 🦚🍯

historical_significance.fact: Janmashtami celebrates the birth of Lord Krishna in Mathura around 3200 BCE. Temples re-enact his birth at midnight and devotees fast the entire day.

title: Ram Navami
description: Celebrating the birth of Lord Rama.

muhurat.description: The time during the middle of the day marking the exact moment Lord Rama was born in Ayodhya.

ritual_steps.title: Morning Snana
ritual_steps.description: Take a holy bath early in the morning and wear clean, fresh clothes.

ritual_steps.title: Cradle Setup
ritual_steps.description: Place a small idol of infant Rama (Ram Lalla) in a beautifully decorated miniature cradle.

ritual_steps.title: Madhyahna Puja
ritual_steps.description: Perform Aarti exactly at noon, the time of His birth, offering Panjiri and Panchamrit.

ritual_steps.title: Ramayana Path
ritual_steps.description: Read or listen to the Ramcharitmanas or chant the Rama Raksha Stotra.

recipes.name: Panjiri
recipes.description: A traditional wheat-based sweet offering commonly made for Ram Navami.
steps: Melt ghee in a heavy-bottomed pan.
Add wheat flour and roast continuously on low flame until it turns golden brown and gives off an aroma.
Turn off the heat, let it cool slightly, then mix in the sugar and dry fruits.
Offer to the deity with a Tulsi leaf before consuming.

dress_guide.description: Yellow is considered highly auspicious for Lord Rama. Devotees often wear yellow or ochre traditional clothing like kurtas, sarees, or dhotis.

notification_templates.discovery: Lord Rama is the 7th avatar of Vishnu, born representing truth and morality. Tap to learn His story.
notification_templates.countdown: Ram Navami is 7 days away! Prepare your home to welcome the birth of Lord Rama.
notification_templates.eve: Tomorrow is Ram Navami. Prepare the cradle and the Panjiri prasadam!
notification_templates.day_of: Happy Ram Navami! May the divine grace of Maryada Purushottam Ram always be with you. 🏹🙏

historical_significance.fact: Ram Navami marks the birth of Lord Rama, the 7th avatar of Vishnu, on the 9th day of Chaitra. Massive Rath Yatras are held across Ayodhya and the country.

title: Raksha Bandhan
description: Celebrating the pure bond between brothers and sisters.

muhurat.description: The best time to tie the Rakhi, avoiding the inauspicious Bhadra period.

ritual_steps.title: Preparation
ritual_steps.description: Prepare the Rakhi thali with an earthen lamp, kumkum, rice, and sweets.

ritual_steps.title: Aarti
ritual_steps.description: The sister performs the Aarti of the brother to ward off the evil eye.

ritual_steps.title: Tying the Rakhi
ritual_steps.description: Sister ties the sacred Rakhi thread on the brother's right wrist.

ritual_steps.title: Vow & Sweets
ritual_steps.description: Brother pledges to protect her, and they feed each other sweets.

recipes.name: Kaju Katli
recipes.description: A diamond-shaped cashew fudge, universally loved for Rakhi.
steps: Boil sugar and water to single-string consistency.
Add cashew powder and stir quickly on low heat.
Wait until it leaves the pan sides. Knead gently.
Roll flat and cut into diamond shapes.

dress_guide.description: Bright ethnic wear. Brothers often wear kurtas, and sisters wear vibrant salwar suits or lehengas.

notification_templates.discovery: Did you know Raksha Bandhan literally translates to 'The Bond of Protection'?
notification_templates.countdown: Only 1 week until Raksha Bandhan! Have you bought the perfect Rakhi and gift?
notification_templates.eve: Tomorrow is Raksha Bandhan! Prepare the thali and the sweets.
notification_templates.day_of: Happy Raksha Bandhan! Celebrate the unbreakable bond between brothers and sisters. 💕

historical_significance.fact: The festival dates to the Mahabharata era when Draupadi tied a strip of her sari to Krishna's wound. He later vowed to protect her — establishing the tradition of sibling bonds.

title: Eid al-Fitr
description: The festival marking the end of the fasting month of Ramadan.

muhurat.description: The special Eid prayer performed in congregation in an open field or large mosque.

ritual_steps.title: Zakat al-Fitr
ritual_steps.description: Give mandatory charity before the Eid prayer to purify those who fasted.

ritual_steps.title: Ghusl
ritual_steps.description: Perform full ablution (Ghusl) before heading out for prayers.

ritual_steps.title: Salat al-Eid
ritual_steps.description: Attend the congregational prayer consisting of two Rakat with extra Takbirs.

ritual_steps.title: Feasting
ritual_steps.description: Enjoy sweet dishes, especially Sheer Khurma, and exchange Eid Mubarak greetings.

recipes.name: Sheer Khurma
recipes.description: A rich vermicelli pudding made with milk, dates, and nuts, traditional for Eid-ul-Fitr.
steps: Fry vermicelli and chopped dry fruits in ghee until golden.
Boil milk and add chopped dates; let it simmer until reduced slightly.
Add the fried vermicelli and sugar.
Cook for 5-7 minutes. Serve hot or chilled.

dress_guide.description: New or clean traditional clothes. Men often wear Shalwar Kameez or Kurta with Attar (non-alcoholic perfume).

notification_templates.discovery: Eid al-Fitr translates to the 'Festival of Breaking the Fast', marking the end of Ramadan.
notification_templates.countdown: Only 7 days left of Ramadan! Let us multiply our good deeds as Eid approaches.
notification_templates.eve: The new moon has been sighted! Tomorrow is Eid al-Fitr. Don't forget your Zakat.
notification_templates.day_of: Eid Mubarak! 🌙✨ May Allah accept your fasting, prayers, and good deeds.

historical_significance.fact: Eid al-Fitr marks the end of Ramadan, the Islamic holy month of fasting. It was instituted by the Prophet Muhammad in 624 CE after the Battle of Badr.

title: Christmas
description: Annual festival commemorating the birth of Jesus Christ.

ritual_steps.title: Advent Preparation
ritual_steps.description: Set up the Christmas Tree and the Nativity Scene (Crib) depicting the birth of Jesus.

ritual_steps.title: Midnight Mass
ritual_steps.description: Attend the solemn Midnight Mass at the local church.

ritual_steps.title: Caroling
ritual_steps.description: Sing joyful carols celebrating the Savior’s birth.

ritual_steps.title: Feasting
ritual_steps.description: Share a grand Christmas dinner with plum cake and exchange gifts.

muhurat.description: The deeply spiritual Midnight Mass marking the exact moment of Christ's nativity.

recipes.name: Traditional Plum Cake
recipes.description: A dense, rich fruit cake synonymous with Christmas in India.
ingredients: Mixed dry fruits and peels (soaked in rum/grape juice for weeks)
steps: Cream butter and sugar.
Add eggs one by one.
Fold in flour, spices, and the soaked fruits.
Bake at 160°C for 60-75 mins until a skewer comes out clean.

dress_guide.description: Festive elegant wear. Red dominates, symbolizing the blood of Christ, while Green represents eternal life.

notification_templates.discovery: The star on top of the Christmas tree represents the Star of Bethlehem that guided the Wise Men.
notification_templates.countdown: Just one week until Christmas! Time to wrap gifts and finish decorating the tree.
notification_templates.eve: Tonight is Christmas Eve! Prepare for the joyous Midnight Mass.
notification_templates.day_of: Merry Christmas! 🎄✨ May the birth of Jesus bring eternal joy and peace to your family.

historical_significance.fact: Christmas commemorates the birth of Jesus Christ in Bethlehem. The date of December 25 was chosen by Pope Julius I around 350 CE to align with existing Roman winter solstice celebrations.

title: Buddha Purnima
description: Celebrating the birth, enlightenment, and death of Gautama Buddha.

muhurat.description: The holiest day in Buddhism observing the birth, enlightenment, and death (Parinirvana) of Gautama Buddha.

ritual_steps.title: Bathing the Buddha
ritual_steps.description: Pour water over the shoulders of a small Buddha statue, symbolizing purification of the mind from greed, anger, and ignorance.

ritual_steps.title: Sutra Chanting
ritual_steps.description: Visit a Vihara (temple) to listen to monks chant the Dharma.

ritual_steps.title: Vegetarianism
ritual_steps.description: Abstain from meat entirely in honor of the Buddha’s teachings of compassion (Ahimsa).

ritual_steps.title: Dana (Giving)
ritual_steps.description: Offer food and basic necessities to monks and the underprivileged.

recipes.name: Kheer
recipes.description: A simple sweet rice pudding. Legend says a woman named Sujata offered a bowl of milk-rice to Buddha right before his enlightenment.
steps: Boil milk until thickened.
Add washed rice and cook on slow heat.
Stir in sugar. Serve simple without extravagant spices representing asceticism.

dress_guide.description: Plain white clothing is highly encouraged to symbolize purity, simplicity, and the shedding of worldly desires.

notification_templates.discovery: Buddha Purnima powerfully commemorates three events: Buddha's birth, his enlightenment, and his final nirvana.
notification_templates.countdown: Buddha Purnima is in 7 days. Reflect on the Four Noble Truths.
notification_templates.eve: Tomorrow is Buddha Purnima. Prepare for a day of peace, giving, and simple vegetarian means.
notification_templates.day_of: Happy Buddha Purnima! ☸️ May you find true inner peace and walk the Middle Path.

historical_significance.fact: Buddha Purnima celebrates Siddhartha Gautama's birth, enlightenment under the Bodhi tree at Bodh Gaya, and Mahaparinirvana — all said to have occurred on the same full moon day.

title: Hanuman Jayanti
description: Celebrating the birth and superhuman strength of Lord Hanuman.

muhurat.description: Lord Hanuman was born at sunrise on the Full Moon day of Chaitra.

ritual_steps.title: Temple Visit
ritual_steps.description: Visit a Hanuman temple early in the morning and offer Sindoor.

ritual_steps.title: Chanting
ritual_steps.description: Read the Hanuman Chalisa, Bajrang Baan, or Sunderkand.

ritual_steps.title: Offerings
ritual_steps.description: Offer Boondi Ladoos, betel leaves, and red flowers.

recipes.name: Boondi Ladoo
recipes.description: Sweet gram flour droplets bound together, a favorite of Lord Hanuman.
steps: Fry tiny droplets of besan batter in ghee.
Soak them in warm sugar syrup.
Add cardamom and seeds, shape into round balls while warm.

dress_guide.description: Red and Saffron are deeply associated with Hanuman, symbolizing energy and strength.

notification_templates.discovery: Hanuman embodies pure devotion (Bhakti), strength (Shakti), and absolute surrender.
notification_templates.countdown: Hanuman Jayanti is arriving in 7 days! Prepare to chant the powerful Chalisa.
notification_templates.eve: Tomorrow is Hanuman Jayanti. Wake up early for the sunrise prayers.
notification_templates.day_of: Happy Hanuman Jayanti! May Bajrangbali grant you boundless strength, courage, and devotion. 📿🐒

historical_significance.fact: Hanuman Jayanti marks the birth of Lord Hanuman, the divine devotee of Lord Rama. He is celebrated for his selfless service, strength, and unwavering devotion in the Ramayana.

title: Gudi Padwa & Ugadi
description: The traditional New Year for Maharashtrians and South Indians.

muhurat.description: The auspicious morning timeframe marking the beginning of the Hindu New Year.

ritual_steps.title: Oil Bath
ritual_steps.description: Take a traditional Abhyanga Snan (oil bath) before sunrise.

ritual_steps.title: Hoisting the Gudi
ritual_steps.description: Erect the Gudi (a pole adorned with a silk cloth, neem leaves, and a copper pot inverted on top) outside the house.

ritual_steps.title: Pachadi/Neem Consumption
ritual_steps.description: Eat a mixture of Neem and Jaggery (or Ugadi Pachadi) signifying the acceptance of life's bitter-sweet moments.

recipes.name: Shrikhand
recipes.description: A Maharashtrian sweet dish made of strained yogurt flavored with saffron and cardamom.
steps: Whisk hung curd and sugar until entirely smooth.
Add saffron soaked in warm milk and cardamom powder.
Garnish with pistachios and serve chilled with puris.

recipes.name: Ugadi Pachadi
recipes.description: A six-taste concoction representing the diverse experiences of life.
steps: Mix all ingredients with water.
Serve a spoonful to family members before meals.

dress_guide.description: New traditional Indian attire. Nauvari sarees for women and Kurta/Dhoti for men.

notification_templates.discovery: The bitter-sweet essence of Neem and Jaggery teaches us to accept life's duality gracefully.
notification_templates.countdown: The Hindu New Year is just a week away! Ready the Gudi and the Shrikhand?
notification_templates.eve: Tomorrow is Gudi Padwa and Ugadi! A glorious start to the new year.
notification_templates.day_of: Happy Gudi Padwa & Ugadi! Wishing you a sweet and prosperous new beginning. 🌿🌟

historical_significance.fact: Gudi Padwa marks the Marathi New Year and is celebrated on the first day of the Hindu calendar month Chaitra. The Gudi flag is hoisted as a symbol of victory and prosperity.

title: Vijayadashami / Dussehra
description: The victory of good over evil, marking the end of Navratri.

muhurat.description: The definitive moment of victory, highly auspicious for starting any new endeavor or performing Shastra Puja.

ritual_steps.title: Shastra Puja
ritual_steps.description: Worship weapons, tools, vehicles, and books as instruments of strength and knowledge.

ritual_steps.title: Aapta Leaves
ritual_steps.description: Exchange Aapta leaves (Banni) referring to them as 'Gold', signifying prosperity.

ritual_steps.title: Ravana Dahan
ritual_steps.description: Witness the burning of the effigies of Ravana, Kumbhakarna, and Meghanada in the evening.

ritual_steps.title: Simollanghan
ritual_steps.description: Cross the boundaries (metaphorical or physical) signifying breaking limits and embarking on new conquests.

recipes.name: Jalebi and Fafda
recipes.description: A classic Gujarati Dussehra pairing uniting sweet and savory textures.
steps: Best sourced fresh from a sweet shop early in the morning.
Enjoy the contrasting crispy salty fafda with syrupy hot jalebis.

dress_guide.description: Festive and formal traditional wear. Royal and bright colors reflecting victory.

notification_templates.discovery: Dussehra marks the day Lord Rama defeated Ravana. It's the ultimate triumph of Dharma over Adharma.
notification_templates.countdown: Only 7 days until Dussehra! Prepare your tools and vehicles for the Shastra Puja.
notification_templates.eve: Tomorrow is Dussehra! The effigies will burn, and truth will prevail.
notification_templates.day_of: Happy Dussehra (Vijayadashami)! May the forces of good continuously guide you to victory. 🏹🔥

historical_significance.fact: Dussehra marks Sri Rama's victory over the demon king Ravana, symbolizing the triumph of good over evil. Effigies of Ravana, Meghnad, and Kumbhakarna are burned across India.

title: Chhath Puja
description: Ancient Hindu festival dedicated to Surya, the sun god.

muhurat.description: The precise timing of offering water to the Setting Sun (Sandhya Arghya) and the Rising Sun (Usha Arghya).

ritual_steps.title: Nahay Khay
ritual_steps.description: Take a holy dip in a river, clean the house, and eat a single sattvic meal.

ritual_steps.title: Kharna
ritual_steps.description: Observe a strict fast for the entire day and break it after sunset with Rasiya (kheer) and Roti.

ritual_steps.title: Sandhya Arghya
ritual_steps.description: Stand in waist-deep water in a river/pond and offer Arghya to the setting sun.

ritual_steps.title: Usha Arghya
ritual_steps.description: Offer Arghya to the rising sun the next morning, completing the 36-hour strict fast.

recipes.name: Thekua
recipes.description: A deep-fried, crisp, sweet wheat biscuit that is the signature Prasad of Chhath Puja.
steps: Mix wheat flour with fennel, coconut, and ghee.
Knead a stiff dough using jaggery water.
Shape into ovals or rounds using a wooden mold.
Deep fry in pure ghee on medium heat until dark golden and crisp.

dress_guide.description: Vratis (fasting individuals) mostly wear unstitched, pure cotton yellow or orange sarees/dhotis.

notification_templates.discovery: Chhath is uniquely dedicated to Chhathi Maiya and Surya Dev (The Sun), thanking them for sustaining life on Earth.
notification_templates.countdown: Chhath Puja begins in 7 days. Time to prepare the wheat and jaggery for Thekua.
notification_templates.eve: Tomorrow is Nahay Khay! Let the sacred 4-day festival of purity and devotion begin.
notification_templates.day_of: Happy Chhath Puja! Stand in the holy waters and offer your prayers to the radiant Sun God. 🌅💧

historical_significance.fact: Chhath Puja is one of the oldest festivals of Bihar and Jharkhand, dedicated to Surya Dev and Chhathi Maiya. Devotees stand in water and offer arghya at sunrise and sunset.

title: Lohri & Makar Sankranti
description: Harvest festivals celebrating the sun's transit into Capricorn.

muhurat.description: Lighting of the Lohri Bonfire at dusk, and the Punya Kaal bath at sunrise for Sankranti.

ritual_steps.title: Bonfire (Lohri)
ritual_steps.description: Light the bonfire in the evening; circumambulate it while tossing til, rewri, and popcorn into the flames.

ritual_steps.title: Holy Snan (Sankranti)
ritual_steps.description: Take a bath in a sacred river during the morning Punya Kaal.

ritual_steps.title: Kite Flying
ritual_steps.description: Fly colorful kites on the terrace, soaking in the gentle winter sun.

ritual_steps.title: Til-Gud Exchange
ritual_steps.description: Exchange sesame and jaggery sweets saying 'Til gud ghya, god god bola'.

recipes.name: Til Ke Ladoo
recipes.description: Sesame and jaggery balls, rich in iron and calcium to keep the body warm.
steps: Dry roast sesame seeds until they pop.
Melt jaggery with water and ghee until frothy.
Mix roasted sesame seeds into the hot jaggery.
Apply drops of water to hands and quickly shape into round balls before it cools.

recipes.name: Sarson Ka Saag & Makki Ki Roti
recipes.description: The iconic Punjabi winter meal served during Lohri.
steps: Boil and blend greens.
Temper with garlic, ginger, and ghee.
Knead cornflour with warm water into discs and cook on a griddle. Serve with massive dollops of white butter.

colors: Black (specifically for Makar Sankranti in Maharashtra)
dress_guide.description: Warm Punjabi festive wear like Phulkari suits for Lohri, and often Black sarees for Sankranti to absorb the sun's heat.

notification_templates.discovery: These festivals mark the end of winter solstice and the Sun's transit into Capricorn (Makara).
notification_templates.countdown: Only a week left until Lohri & Makar Sankranti! Get your kites and peanuts ready.
notification_templates.eve: Tonight is Lohri! Gather around the bonfire. Tomorrow, we fly kites for Sankranti!
notification_templates.day_of: Happy Lohri & Makar Sankranti! May the harvest season bring you boundless prosperity. 🪁🔥

historical_significance.fact: Lohri marks the end of winter and is associated with the harvest of the Rabi crops. Makar Sankranti marks the sun's transit into Capricorn — a scientifically significant astronomical event.

title: Baisakhi, Vishu & Bihu
description: Spring harvest festivals across various Indian states.

muhurat.description: The commencement of the solar New Year across Punjab (Baisakhi), Kerala (Vishu), and Assam (Rongali Bihu).

ritual_steps.title: Vishu Kani
ritual_steps.description: In Kerala, view the auspicious 'Vishu Kani' (golden shower flowers, fruits, gold, mirror) first thing in the morning.

ritual_steps.title: Nagar Kirtan
ritual_steps.description: In Punjab, participate in Gurdwara processions celebrating the formation of the Khalsa panth.

ritual_steps.title: Bihu Dance
ritual_steps.description: In Assam, men and women perform the traditional Bihu dance to the beats of the Dhol and Pepa.

ritual_steps.title: Feasting
ritual_steps.description: Share a grand community or family feast celebrating the Spring harvest.

recipes.name: Til Pitha (Bihu)
recipes.description: A classic Assamese sweet made of glutinous rice stuffed with black sesame and jaggery.
steps: Roast and grind sesame, mix with jaggery.
Spread rice flour thinly on a hot tawa.
Place the stuffing in the center and fold it into a cylindrical roll.

dress_guide.description: Vibrant yellow/orange for Baisakhi, Kerala Kasavu (White & Gold) for Vishu, and Muga silk dresses with red motifs for Bihu.

notification_templates.discovery: Three states, three names, one majestic celebration of the Spring harvest!
notification_templates.countdown: Spring New Year is approaching in 7 days! Prepare for the Bihu dances and Vishu Kani.
notification_templates.eve: Tomorrow is Baisakhi, Vishu, and Bihu. Arrange the Kani and prepare traditional attire.
notification_templates.day_of: Happy Baisakhi, Vishu, & Bihu! Wishing you a bountiful and joyous Solar New Year. 🌾☀️

historical_significance.fact: Baisakhi marks the Sikh New Year and the founding of the Khalsa Panth by Guru Gobind Singh in 1699. It also marks the harvest season in Punjab.

title: Onam
description: Major annual harvest festival celebrated in Kerala.

muhurat.description: The main day of Onam when the spirit of the legendary King Mahabali visits Kerala.

ritual_steps.title: Pookalam
ritual_steps.description: Create intricate floral carpets (Pookalam) at the entrance of the house to welcome King Mahabali.

ritual_steps.title: Onakkodi
ritual_steps.description: Gift and wear new clothes, specifically Kerala Kasavu traditional wear.

ritual_steps.title: Onasadya
ritual_steps.description: Prepare and serve the grand 26-dish vegetarian feast on a plantain leaf.

ritual_steps.title: Vallam Kali
ritual_steps.description: Watch or participate in the spectacular Snake Boat races.

recipes.name: Palada Pradhaman
recipes.description: The king of Onam sweets: a rich milk and rice-flakes dessert.
steps: Wash and soak the rice ada.
Boil milk and sugar until slightly reduced and pinkish.
Add the ada and cook until soft and thickened.
Garnish with cardamom and cashews.

recipes.name: Avial
recipes.description: A thick mixture of 13 vegetables and coconut, seasoned with coconut oil and curry leaves.
ingredients: Mixed local veg (Yam, Plantain, Carrot, Beans, Ash gourd)
steps: Boil vegetables lightly.
Grind coconut and chillies, add to vegetables.
Mix in beaten curd and simmer.
Finish with fresh coconut oil and curry leaves.

dress_guide.description: The traditional Kerala Kasavu saree for women and Mundu for men, symbolizing elegance and purity.

notification_templates.discovery: Onam celebrates the homecoming of the righteous and benevolent Asura King Mahabali.
notification_templates.countdown: Onam is just a week away! Have you planned your 26-dish Onasadya menu?
notification_templates.eve: Tomorrow is Thiruvonam. Prepare the grand Pookalam and get the plantain leaves ready.
notification_templates.day_of: Happy Onam! 🌸🍛 May the spirit of King Mahabali bring prosperity, equality, and joy to your home.

historical_significance.fact: Onam celebrates the homecoming of the legendary King Mahabali, said to visit his people annually from the underworld. It is Kerala's most grandly celebrated harvest festival.

title: Ramadan Begins
description: The beginning of the holy month of fasting, prayer, and reflection.

muhurat.description: The daily period of absolute fasting during the holy month.

ritual_steps.title: Suhoor
ritual_steps.description: Wake up before the Fajr adhan for the pre-dawn meal.

ritual_steps.title: Fasting
ritual_steps.description: Abstain from food, water, and sinful behavior from dawn to dusk.

ritual_steps.title: Iftar
ritual_steps.description: Break the fast immediately at sunset, traditionally with dates and water.

ritual_steps.title: Taraweeh
ritual_steps.description: Perform the special lengthy night prayers in congregation.

recipes.name: Fruit Chaat
recipes.description: A refreshing and hydrating mix of fruits eaten during Iftar.
steps: Chop all fruits evenly.
Sprinkle chaat masala and fresh lemon juice.
Toss gently and serve chilled immediately at Maghrib.

dress_guide.description: Modest and comfortable clothing suitable for long periods of prayer and fasting.

notification_templates.discovery: Ramadan is the month the Quran was first revealed to Prophet Muhammad via Angel Jibril.
notification_templates.countdown: Only 7 days left until the blessed month of Ramadan begins. Prepare your heart and mind.
notification_templates.eve: The crescent has been sighted! Ramadan Mubarak. Tomorrow begins our first fast.
notification_templates.day_of: Ramadan Mubarak! 🌙 May this holy month bring you profound spiritual growth, patience, and peace.

historical_significance.fact: Ramadan is the ninth month of the Islamic lunar calendar, during which Muslims fast from dawn to sunset. It commemorates the first revelation of the Quran to Prophet Muhammad.

title: Eid al-Adha
description: The Feast of Sacrifice, honoring the willingness of Prophet Ibrahim to sacrifice his son.

muhurat.description: The special Eid prayer followed by the Qurbani (sacrifice).

ritual_steps.title: Salat al-Eid
ritual_steps.description: Attend the congregational prayer.

ritual_steps.title: Qurbani
ritual_steps.description: Sacrifice a livestock animal honoring Prophet Ibrahim's obedience to God.

ritual_steps.title: Meat Distribution
ritual_steps.description: Divide the meat into three equal parts: for family, relatives, and the poor.

recipes.name: Mutton Biryani
recipes.description: The quintessential, aromatic meat and rice dish prepared using the fresh Qurbani meat.
steps: Marinate mutton in yogurt and spices.
Parboil rice with whole spices.
Layer meat and rice in a heavy pot (Dum).
Seal and cook on low heat until the meat is tender.

dress_guide.description: Wear your best clothes for the prayer, honoring the spirit of sacrifice and celebration.

notification_templates.discovery: Eid al-Adha honors the willingness of Prophet Ibrahim to sacrifice his son as an act of obedience to God.
notification_templates.countdown: Eid al-Adha is in 7 days. Time to complete the Qurbani preparations.
notification_templates.eve: Tomorrow is Eid al-Adha! Prepare for the morning prayers and the spirit of charity.
notification_templates.day_of: Eid Mubarak! 🌙 May the spirit of sacrifice bring unity, peace, and abundance to your home.

historical_significance.fact: Eid al-Adha commemorates Ibrahim's willingness to sacrifice his son as commanded by God. God provided a ram instead, establishing the tradition of animal sacrifice and charity.

title: Muharram (Ashura)
description: Tenth day of Muharram, a day of remembrance and spiritual reflection.

muhurat.description: A day of profound grief marking the martyrdom of Imam Hussain at Karbala.

ritual_steps.title: Majlis
ritual_steps.description: Attend gatherings to recite elegies (Nohas) commemorating the tragedy of Karbala.

ritual_steps.title: Ashura Procession
ritual_steps.description: Participate in or witness the solemn processions featuring Taziyas.

ritual_steps.title: Fasting
ritual_steps.description: Many Sunnis fast on the 9th and 10th of Muharram.

recipes.name: Haleem / Khichda
recipes.description: A slow-cooked, deeply nourishing meat and grain stew prepared in large cauldrons for community distribution.
steps: Soak grains overnight.
Slow cook meat and grains together until they blend entirely.
Garnish with fried onions, ginger, and lemon.

dress_guide.description: Strictly black clothing for Shia Muslims as a universal symbol of mourning and grief.

notification_templates.discovery: Ashura marks the 10th day of Muharram, solemnizing the supreme sacrifice of Imam Hussain for justice.
notification_templates.countdown: The Day of Ashura is 7 days away. A time for deep reflection and mourning.
notification_templates.eve: Tonight brings the gravity of Ashura. We remember Karbala.
notification_templates.day_of: Observing Ashura. May the legacy of Imam Hussain inspire truth, justice, and courage. 🖤

historical_significance.fact: Muharram is the first month of the Islamic calendar. Ashura (10th of Muharram) marks the martyrdom of Imam Husain ibn Ali at the Battle of Karbala in 680 CE.

title: Guru Gobind Singh Jayanti
description: Birth anniversary of the tenth Sikh Guru, Guru Gobind Singh Ji.

muhurat.description: Celebrating the birth of the 10th Sikh Guru who founded the Khalsa.

ritual_steps.title: Akhand Path
ritual_steps.description: The conclusion of the 48-hour continuous reading of the Guru Granth Sahib.

ritual_steps.title: Nagar Kirtan
ritual_steps.description: A massive holy procession led by the Panj Pyare carrying the Sikh flag (Nishan Sahib).

ritual_steps.title: Langar
ritual_steps.description: Volunteering to cook and serve free food to everyone, regardless of caste or religion.

recipes.name: Kada Prasad
recipes.description: The sacred, incredibly rich sweet offered at the Gurdwara.
steps: Boil water and sugar to make a syrup.
Roast the flour heavily in ghee until dark golden brown.
Carefully pour the syrup into the roasted flour, stirring continuously.
Serve hot with immense devotion.

dress_guide.description: Traditional Sikh attire. Men often wear Turbans in saffron or blue, representing the Khalsa colors.

notification_templates.discovery: Guru Gobind Singh Ji formulated the Five Ks (Panj Kakar) that define the Sikh identity today.
notification_templates.countdown: Guru Gobind Singh Jayanti is in 7 days. Time to volunteer for the Langar preparations.
notification_templates.eve: Tomorrow is the Prakash Utsav. The Akhand Path will conclude tomorrow morning.
notification_templates.day_of: Waheguru Ji Ka Khalsa, Waheguru Ji Ki Fateh! 🙏 Honoring the courage and teachings of the 10th Guru.

historical_significance.fact: Guru Gobind Singh Ji was the 10th Sikh Guru, who founded the Khalsa Panth in 1699, gave Sikhs the Panj Kakars, and compiled the Guru Granth Sahib in its final form.

title: Hola Mohalla
description: Sikh festival celebrated with martial arts exhibitions and poetry.

historical_significance.fact: Hola Mohalla was established by Guru Gobind Singh Ji as an annual gathering of Sikhs to demonstrate military exercises and mock battles, held the day after Holi at Anandpur Sahib.

notification_templates.discovery: Guru Gobind Singh Ji started Hola Mohalla! Discover the warrior spirit of the Khalsa.
notification_templates.countdown: Just {days} days to Hola Mohalla — the festival of Sikh martial arts!
notification_templates.eve: Tomorrow is Hola Mohalla! Prepare to witness the bravery of the Khalsa.
notification_templates.day_of: Waheguruji Ka Khalsa, Waheguruji Ki Fateh! Happy Hola Mohalla!

muhurat.description: The most favorable time for prayers and rituals.

ritual_steps.title: Cleansing
ritual_steps.description: Begin by cleaning the prayer area and taking a purifying bath.

ritual_steps.title: Offerings
ritual_steps.description: Offer fresh fruits, flowers, and light a diya/lamp.

ritual_steps.title: Prayers
ritual_steps.description: Recite the sacred mantras and seek blessings.

title: Guru Nanak Jayanti
description: Celebrating the birth of Guru Nanak Dev Ji, the founder of Sikhism.

muhurat.description: Celebrating the birth of the 1st Sikh Guru and the founder of Sikhism.

ritual_steps.title: Prabhat Pheri
ritual_steps.description: Early morning processions starting from the Gurdwara singing hymns.

ritual_steps.title: Akhand Path
ritual_steps.description: 48-hour continuous reading of the Guru Granth Sahib culminates today.

ritual_steps.title: Kada Prasad & Langar
ritual_steps.description: Distribute the holy Prasad and serve free meals to the community.

recipes.name: Kada Prasad
recipes.description: Sacred wheat flour sweet.
steps: Roast atta in ghee.
Add sugar syrup.
Serve warm.

dress_guide.description: Traditional elegant wear, heads must be covered respectfully.

notification_templates.discovery: Guru Nanak Dev Ji taught three pillars: Naam Japo (chant), Kirat Karo (work honestly), Vand Chhako (share with others).
notification_templates.countdown: Gurpurab is 7 days away. Time to join the Prabhat Pheris early in the morning.
notification_templates.eve: Tomorrow is Guru Nanak Jayanti. The Gurdwaras will be beautifully illuminated.
notification_templates.day_of: Happy Gurpurab! ✨ May the profound teachings of Guru Nanak Dev Ji inspire equity, truth, and compassion.

historical_significance.fact: Guru Nanak Dev Ji, born in 1469, was the founder of Sikhism. He spread the message of Ek Onkar (One God), equality, and service to humanity across the Indian subcontinent.

title: Mahavir Jayanti
description: Celebrating the birth anniversary of Lord Mahavira.

muhurat.description: The celebration of the birth of the 24th and last Tirthankara, Lord Mahavira.

ritual_steps.title: Abhisheka
ritual_steps.description: Ceremonial bathing of Lord Mahavira's idol.

ritual_steps.title: Rath Yatra
ritual_steps.description: A peaceful procession carrying the idol on a chariot.

ritual_steps.title: Ahimsa
ritual_steps.description: Engage strictly in non-violent acts and donate money or food to the poor and animal shelters.

recipes.name: Pure Jain Thali
recipes.description: Food prepared strictly without roots (no onion, garlic, or potatoes) adhering to supreme Ahimsa.
steps: Prepare a simple, non-spicy meal that involves zero harm to microorganisms.

dress_guide.description: Simple clothes. Ascetics wear pure white to signify renunciation of worldly ties.

notification_templates.discovery: Lord Mahavira's core message was 'Live and Let Live', establishing Ahimsa (Non-violence) as the supreme dharma.
notification_templates.countdown: Mahavir Jayanti is 7 days away. Plan your donations for animal welfare.
notification_templates.eve: Tomorrow is Mahavir Jayanti. Prepare for a day of absolute peace and compassion.
notification_templates.day_of: Happy Mahavir Jayanti! 🌿 May the profound principles of Ahimsa and truth guide your life.

historical_significance.fact: Mahavir Jayanti celebrates the birth of Vardhamana Mahavira, the 24th Tirthankara of Jainism, born in 599 BCE in Bihar. He attained Moksha at age 72 and taught the path of Ahimsa.

title: Paryushan Parv
description: Holy period of fasting, repentance, and forgiveness for Jains.

muhurat.description: The most important Jain festival of profound fasting, deep reflection, and seeking forgiveness.

ritual_steps.title: Fasting
ritual_steps.description: Observe intense fasting, drinking only boiled water.

ritual_steps.title: Pratikraman
ritual_steps.description: A daily ritual reflecting on spiritual transgressions and repenting.

ritual_steps.title: Samvatsari (Forgiveness)
ritual_steps.description: On the final day, seek forgiveness from all living beings saying 'Micchami Dukkadam'.

recipes.name: Moong Dal Soup
recipes.description: A very thin, plain lentil soup for breaking intense fasts.
steps: Boil moong dal until completely soft.
Add a pinch of salt and cumin.
Consume warm to ease the stomach.

dress_guide.description: Plain white attire to symbolize purity, detachment, and humility.

notification_templates.discovery: Paryushan ends with 'Micchami Dukkadam', deeply asking forgiveness from everyone we may have hurt, intentionally or unintentionally.
notification_templates.countdown: Paryushan begins in 7 days. Time to mentally prepare for the spiritual fast.
notification_templates.eve: Tomorrow Paryushan begins. May our journey inward be successful.
notification_templates.day_of: Micchami Dukkadam! 🙏 Seeking forgiveness from all forms of life for any transgressions in thought, word, or deed.

historical_significance.fact: Paryushan Parv is an 8-10 day Jain festival of introspection, fasting, and forgiveness. It concludes with Kshamavani — seeking and granting forgiveness to all living beings.

title: Bodhi Day
description: Commemorating the day Siddhartha Gautama achieved enlightenment.

historical_significance.fact: Bodhi Day commemorates the day Siddhartha Gautama attained enlightenment under the Bodhi tree at Bodh Gaya in 596 BCE, becoming the Buddha — the Awakened One.

notification_templates.discovery: Bodhi Day commemorates the Buddha's enlightenment under the Bodhi tree. Discover the path to awakening.
notification_templates.countdown: {days} days until Bodhi Day — a day of peace and mindfulness.
notification_templates.eve: Tomorrow is Bodhi Day! Dedicate a moment to stillness and reflection.
notification_templates.day_of: May you find clarity and peace on this Bodhi Day. Namo Buddhaya!

ritual_steps.description: Begin by cleaning the prayer area and taking a purifying bath.

ritual_steps.title: Prayers

title: Easter Sunday
description: Celebrating the resurrection of Jesus Christ.

historical_significance.fact: Easter celebrates the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead, described in the New Testament as occurring on the third day after his crucifixion. It is the oldest and most important Christian festival.

notification_templates.discovery: Easter celebrates the resurrection of Jesus Christ. Discover the joy and hope of this sacred festival.
notification_templates.countdown: {days} days to Easter Sunday — let hope rise in your heart!
notification_templates.eve: Tomorrow is Easter! May joy and renewal fill your home.
notification_templates.day_of: Happy Easter! He is risen! Wishing you a day filled with hope and joy.

ritual_steps.title: Cleansing

ritual_steps.title: Offerings
ritual_steps.description: Offer fresh fruits, flowers, and light a diya/lamp.

ritual_steps.title: Prayers
ritual_steps.description: Recite the sacred mantras and seek blessings.

title: Republic Day
description: Honoring the date on which the Constitution of India came into effect.

muhurat.description: The unfurling of the National Flag across the country.

ritual_steps.title: Flag Hoisting
ritual_steps.description: Unfurl the Tricolor and sing the National Anthem 'Jana Gana Mana'.

ritual_steps.title: Parade Viewing
ritual_steps.description: Watch the grand Republic Day parade at Rajpath showcasing military might and cultural diversity.

ritual_steps.title: Patriotic Programs
ritual_steps.description: Attend or participate in patriotic songs and dances at schools or community centers.

recipes.name: Tricolor Mithai
recipes.description: Sweets designed in the three colors of the Indian flag.
steps: Divide the sweet mixture into three parts.
Color one orange and one green.
Layer them sequentially and press into a barfi shape.

dress_guide.description: Dress respectfully in traditional or formal wear, often incorporating the colors of the national flag.

notification_templates.discovery: On this day in 1950, the Constitution of India came into effect, turning the nation into a newly formed Republic.
notification_templates.countdown: Republic Day is next week! Prepare your flag and tune into the majestic parade.
notification_templates.eve: Tomorrow is Republic Day! Remember the sacrifices of our freedom fighters and the architects of our Constitution.
notification_templates.day_of: Happy Republic Day! 🇮🇳 Let us pledge to uphold the values enshrined in our Constitution.

historical_significance.fact: Republic Day (January 26, 1950) marks the date when India's Constitution came into effect, replacing the Government of India Act, declaring India a Republic. The Grand Parade at Kartavya Path is iconic.

title: Independence Day
description: Commemorating India's independence from the United Kingdom.

muhurat.description: The hoisting of the National Flag honoring India's freedom.

ritual_steps.title: Prime Minister's Address
ritual_steps.description: Listen to the Prime Minister's speech from the Red Fort.

ritual_steps.title: Flag Hoisting
ritual_steps.description: Hoist the Tricolor with pride at homes and offices.

ritual_steps.title: Kite Flying
ritual_steps.description: Participate in the vibrant tradition of kite flying symbolizing freedom.

recipes.name: Tricolor Pulao
recipes.description: A patriotic rice dish featuring three distinct layers.
steps: Cook three separate batches of rice using the differently colored purees.
Layer them to form a beautiful, patriotic mound of Pulao.

dress_guide.description: The ubiquitous Tricolor themes dominate the attire today.

notification_templates.discovery: The Indian National Anthem 'Jana Gana Mana' was originally composed in Bengali by Rabindranath Tagore.
notification_templates.countdown: Independence Day is only 7 days away. Get your kites ready!
notification_templates.eve: Tomorrow is Independence Day! Hang your flag proudly and prepare for a day of patriotic fervor.
notification_templates.day_of: Happy Independence Day! 🇮🇳 Honoring the brave hearts who won us our freedom.

historical_significance.fact: India gained independence from British rule on August 15, 1947. Pandit Nehru delivered the famous Tryst with Destiny speech, and the national flag was hoisted at the Red Fort.

title: Shivaji Maharaj Jayanti
description: Birth anniversary of the great Maratha emperor, Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj.

muhurat.description: Celebrating the birth of Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj, the great Maratha warrior.

ritual_steps.title: Garland Offering
ritual_steps.description: Offer garlands to the statues and portraits of Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj.

ritual_steps.title: Processions
ritual_steps.description: Participate in magnificent processions featuring traditional Lezim dances.

ritual_steps.title: Powada Recitation
ritual_steps.description: Listen to heroic ballads (Powadas) recounting his valor.

recipes.name: Puran Poli
recipes.description: The classic Maharashtrian sweet flatbread, synonymous with every major celebration in the state.
steps: Make the sweet lentil filling.
Stuff into wheat dough and roast generously with ghee.

dress_guide.description: Traditional Maharashtrian attire with men often wearing the saffron pheta (turban) indicating Maratha pride.

notification_templates.discovery: Shivaji Maharaj established a competent and progressive civil rule with the help of a disciplined military and well-structured administrative organizations.
notification_templates.countdown: Shiv Jayanti is approaching in 7 days! Prepare to honor the great Maratha King.
notification_templates.eve: Tomorrow is Shivaji Maharaj Jayanti. Prepare the garlands and look forward to the Powadas.
notification_templates.day_of: Jai Bhavani, Jai Shivaji! 🚩 Honoring the legendary courage and vision of Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj today.

historical_significance.fact: Shivaji Bhonsle (1630-1680) was the founder of the Maratha Empire. Known for guerrilla warfare, naval supremacy, and progressive administration, he built a kingdom opposing Mughal dominance.

title: Ambedkar Jayanti
description: Birth anniversary of B. R. Ambedkar, the father of the Indian Constitution.

muhurat.description: Honoring the chief architect of the Indian Constitution, Dr. B.R. Ambedkar.

ritual_steps.title: Tributes
ritual_steps.description: Offer floral tributes at statues of Dr. Ambedkar globally.

ritual_steps.title: Pledge for Equality
ritual_steps.description: Reiterate the pledge against caste discrimination and inequality.

ritual_steps.title: Educational Events
ritual_steps.description: Attend seminars emphasizing education and social justice.

recipes.name: Community Feasts
recipes.description: Simple, egalitarian community meals shared by all regardless of background.
steps: Cooked and shared collectively to foster unity.

dress_guide.description: Blue is deeply symbolic of the Dalit movement and Dr. Ambedkar's legacy of equality.

notification_templates.discovery: Dr. Ambedkar held doctorates in economics from both Columbia University and the London School of Economics.
notification_templates.countdown: Ambedkar Jayanti is 7 days away. A time to reflect on equality, liberty, and fraternity.
notification_templates.eve: Tomorrow is Ambedkar Jayanti. Join the nation in honoring the architect of our Constitution.
notification_templates.day_of: Happy Ambedkar Jayanti! 📘 Educate, Agitate, Organize. Let us uphold the vision of true equality.

historical_significance.fact: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar (1891-1956) was the principal architect of India's Constitution. A social reformer and champion of Dalit rights, he fought tirelessly against caste discrimination.

title: Gandhi Jayanti
description: Birth anniversary of Mahatma Gandhi, the father of the nation.

muhurat.description: The birth anniversary of Mahatma Gandhi, observed as the International Day of Non-Violence.

ritual_steps.title: Prarthana
ritual_steps.description: Attend multi-faith prayer meetings and sing 'Raghupati Raghava Raja Ram'.

ritual_steps.title: Shramdaan
ritual_steps.description: Participate in cleanliness drives (Swachhata Abhiyan) offering voluntary labor.

ritual_steps.title: Spinning
ritual_steps.description: Engage in spinning the Charkha, symbolizing self-reliance.

recipes.name: Sattvic Food
recipes.description: Simple, non-spicy vegetarian food reflecting Gandhi's principles of dietetics and Ahimsa.
steps: Cooked simply without excessive spices or oil.

dress_guide.description: Khadi clothing. Wearing handspun Khadi honors his vision of 'Swadeshi' and rural empowerment.

notification_templates.discovery: Gandhi’s philosophy of 'Satyagraha' combined the Sanskrit words 'Satya' (Truth) and 'Agraha' (Firm grasping).
notification_templates.countdown: Gandhi Jayanti is in 7 days. Plan your Shramdaan (cleanliness drive).
notification_templates.eve: Tomorrow we celebrate the Father of the Nation. Prepare to spin the wheel of truth and non-violence.
notification_templates.day_of: Happy Gandhi Jayanti! 🕊️ 'Be the change you wish to see in the world.' Honoring Bapu today.

historical_significance.fact: Gandhi Jayanti marks the birth of Mahatma Gandhi (1869-1948), the Father of the Nation. His philosophy of Satyagraha and Ahimsa inspired India's freedom movement and global movements.

title: Valentine's Day

title: Makar Sankranti
description: A pan-Indian solar festival marking the transition of the sun into Capricorn.

muhurat.description: Lighting of the Lohri Bonfire at dusk, and the Punya Kaal bath at sunrise for Sankranti.

ritual_steps.title: Bonfire (Lohri)
ritual_steps.description: Light the bonfire in the evening; circumambulate it while tossing til, rewri, and popcorn into the flames.

ritual_steps.title: Holy Snan (Sankranti)
ritual_steps.description: Take a bath in a sacred river during the morning Punya Kaal.

ritual_steps.title: Kite Flying
ritual_steps.description: Fly colorful kites on the terrace, soaking in the gentle winter sun.

ritual_steps.title: Til-Gud Exchange
ritual_steps.description: Exchange sesame and jaggery sweets saying 'Til gud ghya, god god bola'.

recipes.name: Til Ke Ladoo
recipes.description: Sesame and jaggery balls, rich in iron and calcium to keep the body warm.
steps: Dry roast sesame seeds until they pop.
Melt jaggery with water and ghee until frothy.
Mix roasted sesame seeds into the hot jaggery.
Apply drops of water to hands and quickly shape into round balls before it cools.

recipes.name: Sarson Ka Saag & Makki Ki Roti
recipes.description: The iconic Punjabi winter meal served during Lohri.
steps: Boil and blend greens.
Temper with garlic, ginger, and ghee.
Knead cornflour with warm water into discs and cook on a griddle. Serve with massive dollops of white butter.

colors: Black (specifically for Makar Sankranti in Maharashtra)
dress_guide.description: Warm Punjabi festive wear like Phulkari suits for Lohri, and often Black sarees for Sankranti to absorb the sun's heat.

notification_templates.discovery: These festivals mark the end of winter solstice and the Sun's transit into Capricorn (Makara).
notification_templates.countdown: Only a week left until Lohri & Makar Sankranti! Get your kites and peanuts ready.
notification_templates.eve: Tonight is Lohri! Gather around the bonfire. Tomorrow, we fly kites for Sankranti!
notification_templates.day_of: Happy Lohri & Makar Sankranti! May the harvest season bring you boundless prosperity. 🪁🔥

historical_significance.fact: Makar Sankranti marks the sun's transition into Capricorn (Makar), ending the winter solstice period. It is one of the few Hindu festivals tied to the solar calendar rather than the lunar calendar.

title: Pongal
description: A multi-day Tamil harvest festival thanking the Sun God for agricultural abundance.

historical_significance.fact: Pongal is Tamil Nadu's 4-day harvest festival thanking the Sun God, rain, and farm animals for a bountiful harvest. The word Pongal means 'to boil over' — symbolizing abundance.

notification_templates.discovery: Pongal is Tamil Nadu's grand 4-day harvest festival. Experience its warmth and abundance!
notification_templates.countdown: {days} days to Pongal! Time to cook the famous sweet Pongal rice.
notification_templates.eve: Tomorrow is Pongal! Prepare your clay pots and sugarcane for the harvest celebration.
notification_templates.day_of: Pongalo Pongal! Happy Pongal! May this harvest season fill your home with abundance.

muhurat.description: The most favorable time for prayers and rituals.

ritual_steps.title: Cleansing
ritual_steps.description: Begin by cleaning the prayer area and taking a purifying bath.

ritual_steps.title: Offerings
ritual_steps.description: Offer fresh fruits, flowers, and light a diya/lamp.

ritual_steps.title: Prayers
ritual_steps.description: Recite the sacred mantras and seek blessings.
